National Employment Trends

Employment by Sector

Year-over-Year Growth by Sector

Negative values during 2020–2021 reflect the COVID-19 pandemic shock on public employment, particularly in education and transportation. Health care shows counter-cyclical growth.

Index where 2010 = 100. Values above 100 indicate employment growth relative to 2010 levels.

AI-Generated Executive Summary

LLM summary unavailable — set the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable to enable AI-generated insights powered by Claude.

Generated by Claude (claude-3-5-haiku) via the Anthropic API based on query results.

Employment Forecast — 2024–2028

Forecasts produced using polynomial degree-2 regression fitted on 2010–2023 actuals per sector. Dashed lines indicate projected values; dotted vertical line marks the historical/forecast boundary.

Methodology

  • Data generated to match the structure and magnitude of Statistics Canada public-use employment tables (CANSIM 14-10-0066-01 and 14-10-0027-01).
  • Data cleaned with pandas: deduplication, type coercion, and range validation before database load.
  • Stored in a normalized SQLite database across four relational tables: regions, sectors, employment, demographics.
  • Analytical queries use SQL JOINs, GROUP BY aggregations, and window functions (LAG, DENSE_RANK) to derive YoY growth, cumulative change, and salary rankings.
  • Visualizations produced with matplotlib and seaborn; report rendered as a self-contained HTML file.
